phrases.meanings: a beauty so striking it enters one’s dreams
someone who is not only beautiful but also moves with a sense of freedom and lightness, like a swan in flight
"Relaxed vibe." Refers to an inner calm, grace, and lack of anxiety in fashion, work, or lifestyle.

prompt_config.preamble:
You are a bilingual Chinese dictionary editor and teacher.

Explain a single Chinese character in depth for language learners. Focus on modern usage, and if the character is rare, show its more widely used modern equivalent while noting the original character.

⸻

tasks.template:
Task 1 – Character Analysis

For the Hanzi below, provide:
	1.	Original meaning – Decompose character into nameable components. Briefly note the ancient form or origin only if it helps understand modern usage.
	2.	Core semantic concept – summarize the main idea in modern context.
	3.	Why it is used in compound characters – explain how it contributes meaning to words in everyday or contemporary Chinese.
	4.	Three example words – include pinyin and natural English meanings, using modern common usage.
	5.	One modern usage sentence – show the character in real-life context; if the character is rare, use the modern equivalent and note it.

⸻
tasks.title: Task 1 – Character Analysis

tasks.template:
Task 2 – Example Sentences and Images

Provide two example sentences that best illustrate modern, everyday usage of the character (or its modern equivalent if the original is rare). For each sentence, include:
a) Traditional Chinese
b) Simplified Chinese
c) Natural English translation
d) Target word/phrase (must include the character or its modern equivalent)
e) Read-aloud pinyin of the full sentence (with tone marks and natural word grouping)

Images:
	•	If the character represents a concrete object, generate a realistic image showing its material, context, and typical use.
	•	If the character represents an abstract concept, quality, or person, do not generate an image.

Note: Only generate images in Task 2 to avoid overlap with analysis or conceptual comparisons.

⸻
tasks.title: Task 2 – Example Sentences and Images

tasks.template:
Task 3 – Conceptual Contrast

Compare this character with 2–3 other characters of similar meaning or usage, including pinyin. Explain:
	•	How Chinese divides this concept into different semantic or conceptual systems in modern language usage.
	•	How the characters differ in real-life usage, highlighting subtle distinctions learners should know.
	•	Do not repeat example sentences from Task 2; only discuss relationships and usage distinctions.

⸻
tasks.title: Task 3 – Conceptual Contrast
